rose.title: Red Rose
rose.description: The red rose, Nehru's signature emblem, reflected his affection for children, elegance, and belief that compassion should accompany leadership.

flag.title: Indian National Flag
flag.description: Represents the birth of independent India and Nehru's role in guiding the nation through its formative years as its first Prime Minister.

desk.title: Prime Minister's Desk
desk.description: Symbolizes the countless decisions, policies, and plans that laid the foundation for modern India's democratic institutions, scientific progress, and economic development.

bust.title: Bronze Bust
bust.description: A commemorative sculpture honoring Jawaharlal Nehru's enduring contribution to nation-building, democracy, education, and scientific advancement.

timeline.title: Early Life
timeline.story: Born in Allahabad into the influential Nehru family, he developed an early curiosity about history, science, and world affairs under private tutors.

timeline.title: Education in England
timeline.story: Studied Natural Sciences at Trinity College, Cambridge, before qualifying as a barrister in London, where liberal democratic ideals deeply influenced his thinking.

timeline.title: Freedom Movement
timeline.story: Joined the Indian National Congress and emerged as one of the leading voices of India's independence movement alongside Mahatma Gandhi.

timeline.title: Years of Imprisonment
timeline.story: Spent nearly nine years in British prisons, where he wrote influential works including 'The Discovery of India' and reflected on India's future.

timeline.title: Tryst with Destiny
timeline.story: On the eve of India's independence, he delivered the historic 'Tryst with Destiny' speech, marking the birth of a free and sovereign nation.

timeline.title: First Prime Minister
timeline.story: Served as India's first Prime Minister, establishing parliamentary democracy and shaping the nation's domestic and foreign policies.

timeline.title: Building Modern India
timeline.story: Championed industrialization, scientific research, higher education, and large infrastructure projects, envisioning India as a modern, self-reliant nation.

timeline.title: Legacy
timeline.story: Remembered as one of independent India's principal architects, whose vision for democracy, secularism, education, and scientific progress continues to influence the nation.
